steps.title: Identify the defining feature of the landscape
steps.content:
The text calls the Fly River delta "remarkably _______ " and then explains why: it is "constantly evolving," changing "in size and shape as the river deposits new sedimentary particles." The blank must mean something like subject to change or capable of changing.
  • Choice A — mutable: Means capable of or subject to change — matches the constant evolution described. ✓
  • Choice B — habitable: Means suitable for living — the text says nothing about whether the delta can support life. ✗
  • Choice C — secluded: Means isolated or hidden — irrelevant to the description of physical transformation. ✗
  • Choice D — homogeneous: Means uniform throughout — contradicts the constant change the text describes. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only "mutable" directly reflects the passage's emphasis on continuous physical transformation. That matches Choice A — mutable.
